---
title: Configuration
---

pycmdcheck is configured via the `[tool.pycmdcheck]` section in your `pyproject.toml`.

## Basic Configuration

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck]
# Exit with non-zero code on these statuses
fail_on = ["error"]

[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
# Enable/disable checks (every check is enabled by default; list only the
# ones you want to turn off or configure).
metadata = true
structure = true
tests = true
linting = true
typing = true
imports = true
license = true
docs = true
# ...plus dependencies, build, formatting, version, py_typed, community, ci,
# changelog, citation, python_versions, urls, doctests. See `pycmdcheck --list`.
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Prefer a [profile](cli.qmd) (`--profile minimal|triage|default|pyopensci|strict`)
to select a curated check set without editing `pyproject.toml`. A profile's
check set overrides the per-check enable flags here.
:::

## Global Options

### `fail_on`

List of statuses that should cause a non-zero exit code.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck]
# Default: only errors
fail_on = ["error"]

# Strict: fail on warnings too
fail_on = ["error", "warning"]

# Very strict: fail on any issue
fail_on = ["error", "warning", "note"]
```

## Check Configuration

Checks can be configured in two ways:

### Boolean (Simple)

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
metadata = true   # Enable
linting = false   # Disable
```

### Dictionary (With Options)

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "pytest" }
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "ruff" }
```

## Per-Check Options

### metadata

Validates `pyproject.toml` has required and recommended fields.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
metadata = true  # No additional options
```

**Required fields**: `name`, `version`
**Recommended fields**: `description`, `readme`, `license`, `requires-python`
**Extended fields** (reported as a NOTE if missing): `authors`, `urls`, `classifiers`

A field declared in PEP 621 `dynamic` (e.g. `dynamic = ["version"]`) counts as
present. Legacy Poetry projects (`[tool.poetry]` with no `[project]` table) are
also recognized.

### structure

Validates package directory structure.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
structure = true  # No additional options
```

Accepts src layout (`src/package/`), flat layout (`package/`), single-file
modules (`package.py`), PEP 420 implicit namespace packages, and
native-extension build backends (maturin/scikit-build/setuptools-rust).

### tests

Runs package tests.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "pytest" }
```

**Options:**

| Option | Values | Default | Description |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `runner` | `"pytest"`, `"unittest"` | `"pytest"` | Test runner to use |
| `args` | list of strings | `[]` | Additional arguments |

**Examples:**

```toml
# Use pytest (default)
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "pytest" }

# Use unittest
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "unittest" }

# With custom args
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "pytest", args = ["-x", "--tb=short"] }
```

### linting

Runs code linting.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "ruff" }
```

**Options:**

| Option | Values | Default | Description |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `tool` | `"ruff"`, `"flake8"`, `"pylint"` | `"ruff"` | Linting tool |
| `args` | list of strings | `[]` | Additional arguments |

**Examples:**

```toml
# Use ruff (default, recommended)
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "ruff" }

# Use flake8
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "flake8" }

# Use pylint
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "pylint" }

# With custom args
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "ruff", args = ["--select", "E,F,W"] }
```

### typing

Runs type checking.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "mypy" }
```

**Options:**

| Option | Values | Default | Description |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `tool` | `"mypy"`, `"pyright"` | `"mypy"` | Type checker |
| `strict` | boolean | `false` | Enable strict mode |
| `args` | list of strings | `[]` | Additional arguments |

**Examples:**

```toml
# Use mypy (default)
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "mypy" }

# Use pyright
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "pyright" }

# Strict mode
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "mypy", strict = true }

# With custom args
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "mypy", args = ["--ignore-missing-imports"] }
```

### formatting

Checks code formatting without modifying files.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
formatting = { enabled = true, tool = "auto" }
```

**Options:**

| Option | Values | Default | Description |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `tool` | `"auto"`, `"ruff"`, `"black"` | `"auto"` | Formatter to check against |
| `args` | list of strings | `[]` | Additional arguments |

With `tool = "auto"` (the default), the formatter is detected from the package:
`[tool.ruff]`/`ruff.toml` → ruff, `[tool.black]` → black. If neither is
configured the check is **skipped** (pyOpenSci does not mandate a formatter).

### imports

Validates all imports can be resolved.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
imports = true  # No additional options
```

### license

Checks for license file.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
license = true  # No additional options
```

Recognizes `LICENSE`, `LICENSE.txt`, `LICENSE.md`, `LICENSE.rst`, `LICENCE`
(and `.txt`/`.md` variants), `COPYING`, `COPYING.txt`, plus suffixed/dual-license
filenames such as `LICENSE-MIT`, `LICENSE-APACHE`, and `COPYING.LESSER`. A file
referenced via `license = {file = "..."}` / `license-files` in `pyproject.toml`
also counts.

### docs

Checks for documentation.

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
docs = { enabled = true, require_readme = true }
```

**Options:**

| Option | Values | Default | Description |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------|
| `require_readme` | boolean | `true` | Require README file |
| `check_docstrings` | boolean | `false` | Check public items for docstrings |
| `check_readme_sections` | boolean | `false` | Check README mentions Installation/Usage |
| `min_docstring_coverage` | float | `0.0` | Minimum docstring coverage % (0 = disabled) |

**Examples:**

```toml
# Basic (require README)
docs = true

# With docstring + section checking
docs = { enabled = true, require_readme = true, check_docstrings = true, check_readme_sections = true }
```

## Complete Example

```toml
[tool.pycmdcheck]
fail_on = ["error", "warning"]

[tool.pycmdcheck.checks]
metadata = true
structure = true
tests = { enabled = true, runner = "pytest" }
linting = { enabled = true, tool = "ruff", args = ["--select", "E,F,W,I"] }
typing = { enabled = true, tool = "mypy", strict = true }
formatting = { enabled = true, tool = "auto" }
imports = true
license = true
dependencies = true
build = true
version = true
py_typed = true
docs = { enabled = true, require_readme = true, check_docstrings = false }
# pyOpenSci / community checks
community = true
ci = true
changelog = true
citation = true
python_versions = true
urls = true
doctests = true
```
